name: Greek Chicken Salad
description: With Feta, Olives & Lemon Dressing
steps: Slice the chicken breast into strips and season with black pepper and dried oregano.
Heat one tablespoon of olive oil in a pan and cook the chicken until golden and cooked through.
Whisk the lemon juice and zest with the remaining olive oil to make a simple dressing.
Drain the cannellini beans and arrange them with the baby spinach, cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion in bowls.
Add the warm chicken on top of the salad.
Scatter over the feta and black olives.
Drizzle with the lemon dressing and toss lightly.
Serve straight away while the chicken is still warm.
tip: Keep the chicken in large strips so it stays juicy and slices neatly through the salad.

name: BBQ Salmon with Spanish Grains
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Cook the wholegrain Spanish grains according to the packet instructions and keep them warm.
Toss the tenderstem broccoli and mange tout with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, salt and pepper.
Spread the vegetables over a lined baking tray and roast until just tender and lightly charred.
Season the salmon with black pepper and place it on the tray beside the vegetables.
Roast until the salmon flakes easily and the vegetables are browned at the edges.
Squeeze over fresh lemon juice and sprinkle with chopped dill.
Fluff the grains and fold through the lemon zest.
Serve the salmon over the Spanish grains with the vegetables alongside.
tip: Preheat the baking tray with the oven so the vegetables start to char as soon as they go in.

name: Chicken Pitta with Tzatziki
description: With Salad, Hummus, Tomatoes & Red Onion
steps: Season the chicken with black pepper, oregano and smoked paprika, then cook it in a hot pan until browned and fully cooked.
Warm the wholemeal pittas so they are soft and ready to fill.
Slice the tomatoes and red onion and toss them with the baby spinach.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for extra freshness.
Split the pittas and spread each one with hummus.
Fill with salad, chicken, feta and red onion.
Spoon in the tzatziki and finish with a squeeze of lemon.
Serve immediately while the pittas are warm.
tip: Warm the pittas just before serving so they do not split when filled.

name: BBQ Chicken Souvlaki
description: With Tzatziki, Greek Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Cut the chicken into strips and marinate with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, oregano, lemon juice and zest, and black pepper.
Cook the chicken on a hot griddle, lined baking tray or air-fryer basket until nicely charred and cooked through.
Prepare a Greek salad with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, feta and olives.
Warm the pittas in a dry pan or oven.
Stir the tzatziki so it is smooth and ready to spoon over the chicken.
Pile the salad into each pitta and top with the chicken strips.
Add a generous spoonful of tzatziki and a little extra lemon if needed.
Serve with the remaining salad on the side.
tip: If time allows, marinate the chicken for 30 minutes before cooking for extra flavour.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Wrap
description: With Hummus, Feta & Baby Spinach
steps: Season the chicken with black pepper, oregano and smoked paprika, then cook until golden and cooked through.
Warm the wraps so they are easier to fold.
Mix the baby spinach with cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Spread each wrap with hummus.
Add the chicken on top of the hummus layer.
Pile in the salad and crumble over the feta.
Finish with lemon juice; the hummus will keep the wrap moist without extra oil.
Roll up tightly and serve.
tip: Keep the filling just off-centre so the wrap rolls more neatly.

name: Turkey & Bean Chilli
description: With Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the basmati rice and keep it covered so it stays fluffy.
Heat the olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and red pepper.
Add the garlic and turkey mince, breaking the meat up as it browns.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin and chilli flakes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer for a few minutes.
Add the red kidney beans and cook until the chilli thickens.
Season to taste and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the chilli over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: Let the chilli simmer uncovered at the end so the sauce reduces cleanly.

name: Tuna & Cannellini Bean Salad
description: With Cherry Tomatoes, Rocket & Lemon Dressing
steps: Season the tuna steaks and sear them quickly in a hot pan.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.
Whisk together lemon juice, lemon zest, olive oil, capers and black pepper.
Slice the cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion.
Toss the rocket with the beans and vegetables in a large bowl.
Slice the tuna and lay it over the salad.
Scatter over the chopped parsley and drizzle with the lemon dressing.
Serve while the tuna is still warm.
tip: Do not overcook the tuna; it should stay tender in the centre.

name: Herb & Lemon Mackerel with Spanish Grains
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Heat the oven and cook the wholegrain Spanish grains according to the packet instructions.
Mix the extra virgin olive oil with the chopped dill, crushed garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, salt and black pepper.
Place each mackerel fillet on its own sheet of foil and spoon over the herb and lemon blend.
Seal each foil parcel loosely, place on a baking tray and bake until the mackerel flakes easily.
Steam or quickly pan-cook the tenderstem broccoli and mange tout so they remain bright and crisp.
Open the foil parcels carefully and spoon the warm herb and lemon juices back over the fish.
Fluff the Spanish grains and check the seasoning of the vegetables.
Serve each mackerel parcel with the Spanish grains and vegetables.
tip: Leave a little air inside each foil parcel so the herb and lemon steam can circulate around the mackerel.

name: Chicken Shakshuka
description: With Spinach, Feta & Wholemeal Pitta
steps: Cook the chicken in a large pan until lightly browned and almost cooked through.
Add extra virgin olive oil, onion and red pepper and soften them for a few minutes.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, cumin and chilli flakes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer until the sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in the baby spinach until wilted, then make two small wells in the sauce and crack in the eggs.
Cover the pan and cook until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still soft.
Scatter the feta over the top and warm the pittas separately.
Serve the shakshuka with the pittas for scooping.
tip: Keep the heat fairly low once the eggs go in so the yolks stay soft.

name: Pesto Basa Pasta
description: With Cherry Tomatoes & Parmesan
steps: Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente, reserving a little pasta water before draining.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and Easy Garlic.
Add the basa fillets and cook gently until they start to flake.
Stir in the green pesto and cherry tomatoes.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the sauce.
Drain the pasta and toss it through the fish and pesto mixture.
Finish with lemon juice, chopped basil and grated parmesan.
Serve immediately while the sauce is glossy and warm.
tip: Fold the fish into the pasta gently so the basa stays in large flakes.

name: Thai Fishcakes
description: With Sweet Chilli Dip & Asian Slaw
steps: Flake the pollock and mix it with the egg, panko, spring onions and coriander.
Season the mixture with salt, pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Shape into small fishcakes and chill briefly if the mixture feels soft.
Mix the Greek yoghurt, honey, chilli flakes and a little lime juice for the dip.
Toss the bagged rainbow slaw with sesame oil, low-salt soy sauce and lime.
Place the fishcakes on a lined baking tray, brush lightly with extra virgin olive oil and bake until crisp, golden and cooked through.
Serve the fishcakes with the sweet chilli dip and the slaw on the side.
Finish with extra coriander if you want a fresher flavour.
tip: Chilling the fishcakes for a few minutes helps them hold together while baking.

name: Herb Crusted Cod
description: With Garlic Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat the oven and chop the courgette, aubergine and red pepper into even pieces.
Toss the vegetables with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until tender and lightly browned.
Mix the panko with chopped parsley, dried thyme and lemon zest.
Brush the cod with Dijon mustard and press the herb crumb onto the top.
Bake the cod until it flakes easily and the topping is golden.
Squeeze lemon over the fish and vegetables.
Serve the cod with the garlic roasted vegetables.
tip: Buying pre-cut Mediterranean vegetables makes this a quick one-tray dinner; otherwise cut everything to a similar size so it roasts evenly.

name: Mediterranean Baked Salmon
description: With Roasted Vegetables & Wholewheat Couscous
steps: Prepare the wholewheat couscous in a separate bowl and fluff it with a fork once tender.
Chop the courgette, aubergine, pepper, cherry tomatoes and red onion into roasting pieces.
Toss the vegetables with extra virgin olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until softened and lightly caramelised.
Season the salmon and place it on top of the tray for the final part of cooking.
Bake until the salmon is just cooked through.
Finish with lemon juice and chopped basil and parsley.
Serve the salmon with the vegetables and couscous.
tip: Add the salmon part-way through roasting so it stays tender and does not dry out.

name: Halloumi & Roasted Vegetable Couscous
description: With Lemon & Fresh Herbs
steps: Cook the wholewheat couscous, then fluff it with a fork and keep it warm.
Chop the vegetables into bite-sized pieces and toss with extra virgin olive oil, smoked paprika and black pepper.
Roast the vegetables until soft and slightly browned.
Slice the halloumi and fry it until golden on both sides.
Drain the chickpeas and mix them into the couscous with the roasted vegetables.
Stir through lemon juice and chopped parsley and mint.
Top with the halloumi slices.
Serve warm with an extra squeeze of lemon.
tip: Pat the halloumi dry and use a separate very hot pan to give it the best golden crust.

name: Lemon Garlic Basa & Prawn Rice
description: With Cherry Tomatoes, Spinach & Fresh Parsley
steps: Cook the wholegrain brown rice according to the packet instructions and keep it covered.
Pat the basa dry, season with black pepper and cook it gently in a large frying pan until it begins to flake.
Move the basa to the edge of the pan, add the extra virgin olive oil and soften the Easy Garlic with the chilli flakes.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook until they soften and begin to burst.
Stir in the prawns and cook until pink, opaque and cooked through, keeping the basa in large flakes.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the tomato and prawn mixture.
Fold in the cooked brown rice, lemon juice and chopped parsley, then season with the measured salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately with an extra squeeze of lemon if desired.
tip: Microwave wholegrain rice makes this a one-pan dinner; fold gently so the rice and basa retain their texture.

name: Chicken Shawarma Bowls
description: With Wholewheat Couscous, Spinach & Garlic Yoghurt
steps: Mix the chicken with extra virgin olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, ground coriander, sumac and one teaspoon of Easy Garlic.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until browned and cooked through.
Prepare the wholewheat couscous according to the packet instructions.
Chop the baby spinach, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Stir lemon juice and the remaining Easy Garlic into the Greek yoghurt.
Fluff the couscous and divide it between bowls.
Add the salad and top with the chicken.
Spoon over the garlic yoghurt and serve.
tip: Toast the spices briefly with the chicken; keep the sumac measured to a quarter teaspoon so its sharp flavour stays balanced.

name: Healthy Chicken Gyros
description: With Homemade Tzatziki, Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Season the chicken with extra virgin olive oil, oregano, smoked paprika and black pepper.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until it is browned and cooked through.
Mix the baby spinach, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion in a bowl.
Warm the pittas.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for a brighter flavour.
Fill each pitta with the salad and chicken.
Add the feta and spoon over the tzatziki.
Serve immediately while the pittas are still warm.
tip: Batch-cook the seasoned chicken for this and the other pitta recipes, then slice it thinly after reheating for quick assembly.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: With Wholemeal Pasta & Parmesan
steps: Mix the turkey mince with the egg, breadcrumbs, parmesan, salt and pepper.
Shape the mixture into meatballs.
Brown the meatballs in olive oil in a large pan.
Add the fresh onion and Easy Garlic and soften them gently.
Pour in the passata and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente.
Wilt the baby spinach into the sauce, then toss through the wholemeal penne, meatballs and basil.
Serve with extra parmesan scattered on top.
tip: Wet your hands slightly when rolling the meatballs to stop the mixture sticking.

name: Chicken Baked Feta Pasta
description: With Roasted Cherry Tomatoes & Fresh Basil
steps: Heat the oven and cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces.
Place the cherry tomatoes, onion and Easy Garlic in a baking dish, drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and season with oregano, chilli flakes and black pepper.
Nestle the block of feta into the centre, arrange the chicken around it and turn the chicken in the seasoned oil.
Bake until the tomatoes are soft, the feta is melting and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente while the chicken and tomatoes roast, reserving a little pasta water.
Mash the feta and tomatoes together, then stir in enough pasta water to make a silky sauce.
Wilt the baby spinach into the sauce, then toss through the penne, cooked chicken and fresh basil.
Check the seasoning and serve immediately.
tip: Cut the chicken into evenly sized pieces so it cooks through at the same time as the tomatoes and feta.

name: Chicken, Spinach & Chickpea Curry
description: With Wholegrain Brown Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the wholegrain brown rice according to the packet instructions and keep it covered while you make the curry.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, then heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large pan.
Brown the chicken on all sides, add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the Easy Garlic, turmeric, cumin, ground coriander, ground ginger and chilli flakes, then stir for one minute.
Stir in the chickpeas and chopped tomatoes and bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
Cook until the sauce thickens and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Add the baby spinach, let it wilt, then season and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the curry over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: Microwave wholegrain rice saves a saucepan; keep the curry at a gentle simmer so the chicken stays tender.

name: Smoky Chicken, Butter Bean & Aubergine Stew
description: With Chorizo, Red Pepper & Rosemary
steps: Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and finely dice the chorizo.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large casserole pan and cook the chorizo briefly until it releases its smoky oils.
Add the chicken and brown it lightly on all sides.
Add the fresh onion, red pepper and diced aubergine, then cook for 6–8 minutes until softened and lightly coloured.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, chopped rosemary and black pepper for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes and drained butter beans, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Stir through the red wine vinegar, check the seasoning without automatically adding salt, then serve in warm bowls.
tip: Dice the chorizo very finely so 40 g flavours the entire stew; it already supplies salt, so taste before adding any more.

name: Turkey & Red Lentil Bolognese
description: With Wholemeal Penne, Spinach & Fresh Basil
steps: Rinse the red lentils under cold water and leave them to drain.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large pan and soften the fresh onion.
Add the turkey mince and cook until lightly browned, breaking it up with a spoon.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, dried oregano and black pepper for one minute.
Add the passata and red lentils with enough water to keep the sauce loose, then simmer until the lentils are tender.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente while the sauce simmers, reserving a little pasta water.
Wilt the baby spinach into the bolognese and loosen with pasta water if needed.
Toss with the penne, stir through the fresh basil and serve.
tip: The lentils thicken as they cook, so add water gradually rather than letting the sauce catch on the bottom of the pan.

name: BBQ Turkey Breast with Butter Bean Salad
description: With Roasted Peppers, Cherry Tomatoes & Parsley
steps: Coat the turkey breast with one tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, oregano and black pepper.
Heat the BBQ for direct cooking and allow the turkey to marinate while it comes to temperature.
Drain and rinse the butter beans, then slice the roasted red peppers, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Combine the beans and vegetables in a large serving bowl.
Whisk the lemon juice and zest with the remaining olive oil and chopped parsley, then fold through the salad.
Cook the turkey on the BBQ until browned and cooked through with no pink remaining.
Rest the turkey for five minutes before slicing it across the grain.
Serve the sliced turkey over the butter bean salad with any remaining lemon dressing.
tip: Flatten thicker parts of the turkey breast to an even depth so the meat cooks at the same rate and stays juicy.

name: BBQ Chicken Caprese Skewers
description: With Pesto Quinoa, Mozzarella & Fresh Basil
steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water while the BBQ heats for direct cooking.
Cut the chicken, courgettes and red peppers into even bite-sized pieces.
Thread the chicken, vegetables and cherry tomatoes onto skewers, then brush with half the pesto mixed with Easy Garlic and black pepper.
Cook the skewers on the BBQ, turning regularly, until the vegetables are charred and the chicken is cooked through.
Cook the quinoa according to the packet instructions, then fluff it with a fork.
Stir the remaining pesto, lemon juice, lemon zest and most of the fresh basil through the quinoa.
Rest the skewers briefly, then serve them over the pesto quinoa.
Tear over the mozzarella and finish with the remaining basil.
tip: Keep the chicken and vegetables similar in size and leave a little space between pieces so the BBQ heat can circulate.

name: Healthy Chicken, Chorizo & Seafood Paella
description: With Prawns, Mussels & Peas
steps: Warm the low-salt chicken stock and stir in the turmeric while you prepare the other ingredients.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a wide paella pan, cook the finely diced chorizo briefly, then add and lightly brown the chicken.
Add the fresh onion and red pepper and cook until softened.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, black pepper and paella rice until every grain is coated.
Add the chopped tomatoes and seasoned stock, spread the rice evenly and simmer gently without stirring until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Nestle the cleaned mussels into the rice, cover loosely and cook until they begin to open.
Add the prawns and frozen peas and cook until the prawns are opaque, the mussels are open and the chicken is cooked through; discard any mussels that remain closed.
Rest for five minutes, then finish with lemon juice, lemon wedges and chopped parsley.
tip: Use the widest pan you have and resist stirring after adding the stock; the thin rice layer cooks more evenly and can develop a lightly toasted base.

name: Teriyaki Pork Tenderloin Stir-Fry
description: With Brown Rice, Broccoli & Mange Tout
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Whisk the low-salt soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, grated ginger, crushed garlic, cornflour and two tablespoons of water into a smooth teriyaki sauce.
Trim the pork tenderloin and slice it thinly across the grain so it cooks quickly.
Heat half the sesame oil in a large non-stick wok and stir-fry the pork over a high heat until browned and just cooked through, then transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining sesame oil, broccoli, sliced red pepper and mange tout and stir-fry for four to five minutes, adding a splash of water to help the broccoli steam.
Return the pork to the wok and pour in the teriyaki sauce.
Toss everything for one to two minutes until glossy and thickened, checking that the pork is piping hot.
Divide the brown rice between bowls, top with the stir-fry and finish with sliced spring onions.
tip: Slice the pork while it is slightly chilled; thin, even pieces brown fast without drying out.

name: Venison Steak with Blackberry Sauce
description: With Roasted Carrots, Parsnips & Sweet Potato
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan and place a large baking tray inside to heat.
Cut the carrots, parsnips and sweet potato into slim, even batons and toss with half the olive oil, rosemary and black pepper.
Spread the vegetables on the hot tray and roast for 25 to 30 minutes, turning once, until tender and caramelised.
Pat the venison dry, season with black pepper and rub with the remaining olive oil.
Sear the steaks in a very hot frying pan for two to three minutes per side for medium-rare, or to your liking, then rest loosely covered.
Lower the heat, add the finely chopped shallot to the same pan and cook for two minutes.
Add the blackberries, balsamic vinegar, half a beef stock cube and 150ml water; simmer for five minutes, lightly crushing some berries until the sauce is glossy.
Slice the rested venison across the grain and serve with the roasted roots and warm blackberry sauce.
tip: Venison is very lean, so rest it well and avoid cooking beyond medium if you want it tender.

name: BBQ Sirloin Steak with Chimichurri
description: With Charred Pak Choi & Sweet Potato Fries
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan and line a large tray with baking paper.
Cut the sweet potatoes into slim fries, toss with one tablespoon of olive oil, smoked paprika and black pepper, then roast for 25 to 30 minutes, turning halfway.
Finely chop the parsley and coriander and mix with the crushed garlic, red wine vinegar, chilli flakes, one tablespoon of olive oil and a tablespoon of water to make the chimichurri.
Trim any thick outer fat from the sirloin, pat dry and season lightly with salt and black pepper.
Cook the steak on a very hot barbecue or griddle for two to four minutes per side, depending on thickness and how you like it cooked.
Transfer the steak to a board, spoon over a little chimichurri and rest for five to eight minutes.
Halve the pak choi lengthways and char it cut-side down for two to three minutes, then turn and cook until just tender.
Slice the steak across the grain and serve with the pak choi, sweet potato fries and remaining chimichurri.
tip: Sirloin brings plenty of flavour but stays relatively lean when the outer fat is trimmed before cooking.

name: Healthy Pork Satay Bowls
description: With Brown Rice, Crunchy Slaw & Quick-Pickled Cucumber
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Use a vegetable peeler to make cucumber ribbons, then toss them with one tablespoon of rice vinegar and half the honey and set aside to pickle.
Finely shred the red cabbage, grate the carrot and slice the spring onions, then toss together with half the lime juice.
Whisk the peanut butter, low-salt soy sauce, remaining rice vinegar and honey, grated ginger, crushed garlic, remaining lime juice and four tablespoons of warm water until smooth.
Trim the pork tenderloin, slice thinly and coat with one-third of the satay sauce.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a non-stick frying pan and cook the pork for five to seven minutes, turning, until browned and cooked through.
Add the remaining satay sauce to the pan and toss for one minute until hot and lightly thickened.
Build the bowls with brown rice, crunchy slaw, drained pickled cucumber and pork satay, then scatter over the spring onions.
tip: Loosen natural peanut butter with warm water before adding it to the pan for a creamy sauce without coconut milk.

name: Angel's Wings Chicken Grain Bowls
description: With Mediterranean Vegetables & Garlic Yoghurt
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan and line a large roasting tray.
Cut the courgette, aubergine, red pepper and red onion into bite-sized pieces, add the cherry tomatoes and toss everything with half the olive oil.
Roast the vegetables for 20 to 25 minutes, turning once, until tender and browned at the edges.
Coat the chicken breasts with the Angel's Wings rub and remaining olive oil, then grill or pan-cook for five to seven minutes per side until cooked through.
Cook the bulgur wheat according to the packet instructions, drain well and fluff with half the lemon juice and chopped parsley.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with the crushed garlic, remaining lemon juice and enough cold water to make a spoonable sauce.
Rest the chicken for five minutes, then slice it across the grain.
Divide the bulgur and roasted vegetables between bowls, add the chicken and spoon over the garlic yoghurt sauce.
tip: If your Angel's Wings rub contains salt, do not add extra seasoning until you have tasted the finished dish.

name: Pork Fillet Ragù
description: With Wholewheat Tagliatelle & Hidden Vegetables
steps: Trim the pork tenderloin, cut it into chunks and pulse briefly in a food processor until coarsely minced, taking care not to turn it into a paste.
Heat the olive oil in a wide pan and cook the finely chopped onion, carrot, celery and mushrooms for eight minutes until soft and lightly coloured.
Add the pork and cook over a medium-high heat for five minutes, breaking it up, until no pink remains.
Stir in the crushed garlic, tomato purée and dried oregano and cook for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, crumbled beef stock cube and 200ml water, then simmer uncovered for 15 to 20 minutes until rich and thick.
Meanwhile, cook the wholewheat tagliatelle according to the packet instructions until al dente, reserving a mugful of pasta water.
Toss the pasta with the ragù, adding a splash of reserved water if needed, and check the seasoning.
Divide between warm bowls and finish with fresh basil and a light grating of Parmesan.
tip: Pulse the pork in short bursts; a coarse texture makes a meatier ragù and prevents the lean fillet becoming dense.

name: Smoky Beef & Black Bean Stuffed Peppers
description: With Quinoa, Tomatoes & Lime Yoghurt
steps: Heat the oven to 210°C/190°C fan and line a roasting tray.
Halve the peppers lengthways through the stalks, remove the seeds and roast cut-side up for 12 minutes.
Cook the quinoa according to the packet instructions, then drain well.
Heat the olive oil in a non-stick pan and cook the chopped onion for five minutes, then add the beef and brown it thoroughly, breaking it into small pieces.
Stir in the crushed garlic, smoked paprika, cumin and tomato purée, then add the chopped tomatoes and simmer for eight minutes.
Fold the drained black beans and cooked quinoa into the beef mixture and spoon generously into the pepper halves.
Return the peppers to the oven for 12 to 15 minutes until tender and the filling is piping hot.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with lime juice, then spoon it over the peppers and finish with chopped coriander.
tip: Choose broad, evenly shaped peppers so they sit flat and hold plenty of filling without tipping.

name: Healthy Traditional Beef Chilli Con Carne
description: With Kidney Beans, Peppers & Brown Rice
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pan and cook the finely chopped onion, red pepper and grated carrot for eight minutes until soft.
Add the lean beef mince and cook over a medium-high heat until deeply browned, breaking it up with a wooden spoon.
Stir in the crushed garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, chilli flakes and cinnamon and cook for one minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato purée, chopped tomatoes, crumbled beef stock cube and 250ml water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Part-cover and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has reduced and the vegetables are tender.
Rinse and drain the kidney beans, stir them into the chilli and simmer uncovered for a further 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions and drain well.
Serve the chilli over the rice with Greek yoghurt and chopped coriander.
tip: Browning the mince properly before adding liquid creates the slow-cooked flavour this quicker chilli needs.

name: Fragrant Chickpea & Courgette Curry
description: With Zesty Parsley Brown Rice
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions until tender, then drain well.
Finely chop the shallot and cut the courgette into bite-sized half-moons.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a wide pan and cook the shallot and courgette for five minutes until lightly golden.
Add the Easy Garlic, curry powder, cumin, ground coriander, turmeric and cinnamon and stir for one minute until fragrant.
Add the drained chickpeas, chopped tomatoes and light coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes until the sauce has thickened and the courgette is tender.
Fold the lime zest, half the lime juice and most of the chopped parsley through the brown rice.
Season the curry with the remaining lime juice and serve over the zesty rice with the remaining parsley.
tip: Curry powder with cumin, coriander, turmeric and a little cinnamon gives a fragrant East African-style profile without needing a specialist Zanzibar blend.

name: Marmite & Peanut Butter Pork Udon
description: With Spinach, Spring Onion & Roasted Peanuts
steps: Whisk the peanut butter, Marmite, chilli jam, low-salt soy sauce, Easy Ginger, Easy Garlic and five tablespoons of hot water until smooth.
Cook the wholemeal udon according to the packet instructions, then drain and reserve a little cooking water.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a large non-stick frying pan or wok.
Add the lean pork mince and cook over a high heat for six to seven minutes, breaking it up, until browned and cooked through.
Add the sliced spring onions, reserving a few green slices for serving, and cook for one minute.
Add the spinach and toss until just wilted.
Add the noodles and sauce and toss for two minutes, loosening with reserved cooking water until glossy but not heavy.
Divide between bowls and finish with the reserved spring onion and roughly chopped roasted peanuts.
tip: Marmite is powerful and salty, so start with two level teaspoons and use low-salt soy sauce to keep the seasoning balanced.

name: Crispy Chicken Bibimbap
description: With Pak Choi, Carrot, Egg & Brown Rice
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Mix the low-salt soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, Easy Ginger and Easy Garlic to make a quick alternative to ketjap manis.
Slice the chicken into thin strips, coat with the cornflour and half the sauce, and set aside for five minutes.
Heat one teaspoon of sesame oil in a non-stick pan and cook the chicken until crisp at the edges and completely cooked through.
Remove the chicken, then stir-fry the shredded carrot and sliced pak choi in the remaining sesame oil until just tender.
Fry the eggs in the same pan until the whites are set and the yolks remain soft, or cook to your preference.
Divide the rice between bowls and arrange the chicken, carrot and pak choi separately over the top.
Add an egg to each bowl, drizzle with the remaining sauce and finish with sliced spring onions.
tip: A light cornflour coating gives the chicken crisp edges with very little oil, while soy and honey recreate ketjap manis using common pantry ingredients.

name: Korean-Style Gochujang Turkey
description: With Spinach Rice, Cucumber & Egg
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, adding the spinach for the final two minutes so it wilts into the rice.
Whisk the gochujang, low-salt soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar and two tablespoons of water into a smooth sauce.
Cut the cucumber into thin half-moons and toss with a teaspoon of the sauce, then set aside.
Heat one teaspoon of sesame oil in a non-stick frying pan and brown the turkey mince for six to eight minutes, breaking it into small pieces.
Pour in the remaining gochujang sauce and cook for two minutes until the turkey is glossy and fully cooked.
Fry the eggs in the remaining sesame oil until the whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your liking.
Divide the spinach rice between bowls and top with the gochujang turkey, cucumber and eggs.
Finish with sesame seeds and finely sliced spring onions.
tip: Lean turkey mince takes on the gochujang glaze just as well as pork while reducing saturated fat and keeping the bowl high in protein.

name: Sesame, Honey & Lime Basa
description: With Sweet Potato Wedges & Fresh Coriander
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan and line a large tray with baking paper.
Cut the sweet potatoes into slim wedges, toss with half the rapeseed oil and black pepper and roast for 25 to 30 minutes, turning once.
Mix the honey, lime zest and juice, low-salt soy sauce, Easy Ginger and Easy Garlic.
Pat the basa dry, dust lightly with cornflour and black pepper and brush with half the honey-lime mixture.
Heat the remaining oil in a non-stick frying pan and cook the basa for three to four minutes per side until golden and opaque.
Add the remaining honey-lime mixture to the pan for the final minute and turn the fish carefully until lightly glazed.
Scatter the sesame seeds over the fish and check that it flakes easily at its thickest point.
Serve with the sweet potato wedges and finish with chopped coriander and sliced spring onion.
tip: Add the honey glaze only near the end of cooking so it turns glossy without catching or burning.

name: Pork & Refried Black Bean Tacos
description: With Zesty Apple Slaw & Chipotle Yoghurt
steps: Finely shred the red cabbage, cut the apple into matchsticks and thinly slice half the red onion.
Toss the cabbage, apple and sliced onion with the red wine vinegar and half the lime juice and set aside.
Mash the drained black beans with half the Easy Garlic, half the chipotle paste and four tablespoons of water.
Warm the bean mixture in a small pan for five minutes, stirring until creamy but still textured.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a frying pan and cook the remaining chopped onion and pork mince until browned and completely cooked.
Add the smoked paprika, low-salt soy sauce and remaining chipotle and Easy Garlic and cook for two minutes.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with the remaining lime juice and warm the tortillas in a dry pan.
Spread each tortilla with refried beans, add the pork and apple slaw and finish with chipotle-lime yoghurt.
tip: Keep the slaw undressed until shortly before serving so the apple stays crisp and fresh.

name: Chicken & Refried Black Bean Tacos
description: With Zesty Apple Slaw & Chipotle Yoghurt
steps: Finely shred the red cabbage, cut the apple into matchsticks and thinly slice half the red onion.
Toss the cabbage, apple and sliced onion with the red wine vinegar and half the lime juice and set aside.
Mash the drained black beans with half the Easy Garlic, half the chipotle paste and four tablespoons of water.
Warm the bean mixture in a small pan for five minutes, stirring until creamy but still textured.
Slice the chicken thinly, then cook it with the remaining chopped onion in the rapeseed oil until golden and cooked through.
Add the smoked paprika, low-salt soy sauce and remaining chipotle and Easy Garlic and toss for two minutes.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with the remaining lime juice and warm the tortillas in a dry pan.
Spread each tortilla with refried beans, add the chicken and apple slaw and finish with chipotle-lime yoghurt.
tip: Thin chicken strips cook quickly and stay juicy, making this the leanest meat version of the taco recipe.

name: Chipotle Basa & Refried Black Bean Tacos
description: With Zesty Apple Slaw & Lime Yoghurt
steps: Finely shred the red cabbage, cut the apple into matchsticks and thinly slice half the red onion.
Toss the cabbage, apple and sliced onion with the red wine vinegar and half the lime juice and set aside.
Mash the drained black beans with half the Easy Garlic, half the chipotle paste and four tablespoons of water.
Warm the bean mixture in a small pan for five minutes, stirring until creamy but still textured.
Pat the basa dry and coat with smoked paprika, low-salt soy sauce and the remaining chipotle and Easy Garlic.
Cook the basa in the rapeseed oil for three to four minutes per side until golden and flaking, then break into large pieces.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with the remaining lime juice and warm the tortillas in a dry pan.
Spread each tortilla with refried beans, add the basa and apple slaw and finish with lime yoghurt.
tip: Use less chipotle in the yoghurt for the fish version so its delicate flavour is not overwhelmed.

name: Teriyaki Basa with Sesame Greens
description: With Brown Rice, Mange Tout & Sugar Snap Peas
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Whisk the low-salt soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, Easy Ginger, Easy Garlic, cornflour and three tablespoons of water into a teriyaki sauce.
Pat the basa dry and brush each fillet with a spoonful of the sauce.
Heat one teaspoon of sesame oil in a non-stick frying pan and cook the basa for three to four minutes per side until golden and opaque.
Transfer the fish to a warm plate, taking care to keep the fillets intact.
Add the remaining sesame oil, mange tout and sugar snap peas to the pan and stir-fry for three minutes until bright and crisp-tender.
Pour in the remaining sauce and simmer briefly until glossy, then return the fish to coat lightly.
Serve with the brown rice and sesame greens and finish with sesame seeds and sliced spring onions.
tip: Basa is delicate, so turn it only once and add it back to the glaze at the very end.

name: Chicken Tikka Flatbread Bowls
description: With Spiced Chickpeas, Crunchy Salad & Mint Yoghurt
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C fan and line a baking tray.
Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces and toss with half the yoghurt, half the Easy Garlic, the curry powder and half the lime juice.
Drain the chickpeas, pat dry and toss with the smoked paprika, cumin and one teaspoon of rapeseed oil.
Spread the chicken and chickpeas on the tray and roast for 16 to 20 minutes, turning once, until the chicken is cooked and the chickpeas are lightly crisp.
Dice the cucumber and tomatoes, finely slice the red onion and toss together with a squeeze of lime.
Chop the mint and stir it into the remaining yoghurt with the remaining Easy Garlic and lime juice.
Toast the wholemeal pitta, cut it into wedges and divide it between two bowls with the salad.
Add the tikka chicken and chickpeas, then spoon over the mint yoghurt and serve.
tip: Roasting the chickpeas beside the chicken adds texture without needing extra oil or salt.

name: Sticky Korean Chicken Noodle Salad
description: With Crunchy Slaw, Cucumber & Sesame-Lime Dressing
steps: Cook the wholemeal udon according to the packet instructions, rinse under cold water and drain well.
Whisk the gochujang, low-salt soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, lime juice, Easy Ginger, Easy Garlic and two tablespoons of water.
Slice the chicken thinly and coat it with one third of the dressing.
Heat the sesame oil in a large non-stick frying pan and cook the chicken for six to eight minutes until browned and cooked through.
Pour another third of the dressing into the pan and toss for one minute until the chicken is glossy and sticky.
Cut the cucumber into ribbons and finely slice the spring onions.
Toss the noodles with the slaw, cucumber, most of the spring onions and the remaining dressing.
Divide between bowls, top with the sticky chicken and finish with sesame seeds and the remaining spring onions.
tip: Rinsing the noodles stops them clumping and keeps this warm salad fresh and crisp.

name: Chicken & Prawn Jambalaya
description: With Peppers, Celery & a Touch of Chorizo
steps: Dice the chicken, chorizo, onion, peppers and celery into small, even pieces.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a large lidded pan and cook the chorizo and chicken for five minutes until lightly coloured.
Add the onion, peppers and celery and cook for five minutes until beginning to soften.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, thyme and cayenne and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the brown rice, chopped tomatoes and low-salt stock, stir once and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 20 to 25 minutes, or according to the rice packet, stirring once near the end and adding a splash of water if needed.
Stir in the prawns and peas, cover again and cook for four to five minutes until the prawns are pink and the rice is tender.
Rest off the heat for five minutes, fluff the rice and serve in warm bowls.
tip: A small amount of finely diced chorizo flavours the whole pan while keeping saturated fat and salt in check.

name: Moroccan Chicken & Apricot Tagine
description: With Chickpeas, Carrots & Fragrant Spices
steps: Cut the chicken into large chunks, slice the carrots and onion and halve the dried apricots.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a large lidded pan and brown the chicken for four minutes.
Add the onion and carrots and cook for five minutes, stirring regularly.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon and turmeric for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes, low-salt stock, drained chickpeas and apricots and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook gently for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked and the carrots are tender, then uncover briefly if the sauce needs thickening.
Prepare the wholewheat couscous according to the packet instructions and fluff with half the lemon juice and chopped coriander.
Brighten the tagine with the remaining lemon juice and serve it over the couscous.
tip: Keeping the apricots to a small handful gives gentle sweetness while the carrots and spices do most of the work.

name: Turkey Enchilada Stuffed Peppers
description: With Black Beans, Sweetcorn & Lime Yoghurt
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C fan, halve the peppers lengthways and remove the seeds.
Place the pepper halves cut-side up in a baking dish, add a splash of water and bake for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat the rapeseed oil in a frying pan and cook the turkey mince and diced red onion for six minutes, breaking up the mince.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, cumin and chipotle paste and cook for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, drained black beans and sweetcorn and simmer for five minutes until thick.
Spoon the filling into the pepper halves, scatter over the cheddar and bake for 12 to 15 minutes until bubbling.
Mix the Greek yoghurt with the lime zest and juice.
Serve two stuffed pepper halves per person with the lime yoghurt spooned over the top.
tip: A small amount of mature cheddar delivers plenty of flavour; choose a reduced-fat version if available.

name: Turkey Keema with Peas & Spinach
description: With Brown Rice, Tomatoes & Warming Spices
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a large frying pan and soften the finely diced onion for five minutes.
Add the turkey mince and cook for six minutes, breaking it into small pieces until lightly browned.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, Easy Ginger, cumin, coriander, turmeric and cayenne for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes and low-salt stock and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
Stir in the peas and cook for three minutes, then add the spinach a handful at a time until wilted.
Squeeze in the lime juice and stir through most of the chopped coriander.
Serve the turkey keema with the brown rice and finish with the remaining coriander.
tip: Let the tomato sauce reduce before adding the vegetables so the finished keema stays rich rather than watery.

name: Pork Souvlaki with Lemon Potatoes
description: With Greek Salad & Herby Yoghurt
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C fan, halve the new potatoes and place them on a large baking tray.
Toss the potatoes with one tablespoon of olive oil, half the oregano, half the Easy Garlic, half the lemon juice and black pepper, then roast for 25 to 30 minutes.
Cut the pork tenderloin into chunks and toss with the remaining oregano, smoked paprika, Easy Garlic, lemon juice and olive oil.
Thread the pork onto skewers, or leave as loose chunks if preferred.
Cook the pork in a hot griddle or non-stick frying pan for eight to 10 minutes, turning regularly, until browned and cooked through.
Halve the tomatoes, dice the cucumber and finely slice the red onion, then toss them together.
Chop the parsley and mix it with the Greek yoghurt and a spoonful of water.
Serve the pork with the lemon potatoes, Greek salad and herby yoghurt.
tip: Pork tenderloin is naturally lean; cut it into even chunks so it cooks quickly without drying out.

name: Smoky Pork, Pepper & Butter Bean Casserole
description: With Spinach, Tomatoes & Rosemary
steps: Trim the pork tenderloin and cut it into bite-sized pieces, then slice the peppers and onion.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a large casserole pan and brown the pork for four minutes, then transfer it to a plate.
Add the onion and peppers to the same pan and cook for six minutes until softened.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, tomato purée, smoked paprika, rosemary and chilli flakes for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, low-salt stock and drained butter beans and bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the pork to the pan and simmer uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes until cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the spinach a handful at a time until wilted and add a splash of water if the casserole is too thick.
Taste, season with black pepper if needed and serve scattered with chopped parsley.
tip: Browning the pork first builds flavour, but returning it only for the final simmer keeps this lean cut tender.

name: Mediterranean Cod & Chickpea Traybake
description: With Courgette, Tomatoes, Olives & Lemon
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C fan and line a large, shallow baking tray.
Slice the courgette, red onion and pepper and add them to the tray with the tomatoes and drained chickpeas.
Toss the vegetables and chickpeas with the olive oil, Easy Garlic, oregano, smoked paprika, lemon zest and black pepper.
Roast for 15 minutes, turning the vegetables once.
Pat the cod dry, place it among the vegetables and squeeze over half the lemon juice.
Scatter the olives around the tray and return it to the oven for 10 to 12 minutes until the cod flakes easily.
Squeeze over the remaining lemon juice and gently stir the tray juices through the chickpeas.
Divide between two plates and finish with plenty of chopped parsley.
tip: Rinse the chickpeas and olives before using them to reduce their salt while keeping their flavour and texture.

name: Thai Green Basa Curry
description: With Peppers, Green Beans, Spinach & Brown Rice
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the packet instructions, then drain and keep warm.
Cut the basa into large chunks, slice the pepper and spring onions and trim the green beans.
Heat the rapeseed oil in a deep non-stick frying pan and cook the pepper and beans for four minutes.
Add the Thai green curry paste, Easy Ginger, Easy Garlic and the white parts of the spring onions and stir for one minute.
Pour in the light coconut milk and 150ml water, bring to a gentle simmer and cook for five minutes.
Nestle in the basa and simmer gently for five to six minutes without vigorous stirring, until the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Fold in the spinach, lime juice and fish sauce and cook just until the spinach has wilted.
Serve with the brown rice and finish with coriander and the green spring onion tops.
tip: Curry pastes vary in heat and salt, so start with less if needed and use lime and coriander to lift the finished sauce.

name: Cajun Salmon with Sweet Potato & Corn Salsa
description: With Tomatoes, Lime & Coriander
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C fan and line a large baking tray.
Cut the sweet potato into 2cm cubes, toss with one teaspoon of rapeseed oil and half the smoked paprika, then roast for 15 minutes.
Mix the remaining smoked paprika with the sweet paprika, garlic granules, thyme and cayenne.
Rub the salmon with the remaining rapeseed oil and the spice mix, then place it on the tray beside the sweet potato.
Roast everything for a further 10 to 12 minutes until the salmon flakes easily and the sweet potato is tender.
Meanwhile, drain the sweetcorn, halve the tomatoes, finely dice the red onion and chop the coriander.
Toss the salsa ingredients with the lime zest and juice, then stir a spoonful of lime juice into the Greek yoghurt.
Serve the Cajun salmon with the roasted sweet potato, corn salsa and lime yoghurt.
tip: Salmon's unsaturated fats make it a heart-friendly choice; the spice crust supplies bold flavour without added salt.
